title: Roezliella lehmanniana
intro: Roezliella lehmanniana, also known as Chrysolaena lehmanniana, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Roezliella lehmanniana include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared in teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in topical applications to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Roezliella lehmanniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is commonly known as the "melastome" or "memecyclid" family. Melastomataceae is a large and diverse family of plants, with over 150 genera and 5,000 species worldwide. The family is native to tropical and subtropical regions, particularly in Asia, Africa, and the Americas. Roezliella lehmanniana is one of the many species found within this family.
